paper_title: Mutagenic Effects of Ethyl Methane Sulphonate on Urginea indica Kunth

methods.method_name: Bulb Treatment with Ethyl Methane Sulphonate
methods.description: This method describes the process of treating Urginea indica Kunth bulbs with different concentrations of Ethyl Methane Sulphonate to study its mutagenic effects.

steps.description: Five different concentrations of Ethyl Methane Sulphonate (0.1%, 0.2%, 0.3%, 0.4% and 0.5%) were prepared under aseptic conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare Ethyl Methane Sulphonate solutions at specified concentrations

steps.description: Fresh and healthy Urginea indica Kunth bulbs of uniform size were treated with these five concentrations for six hours.
steps.searchable_summary: treat bulbs with Ethyl Methane Sulphonate solutions for six hours

steps.description: The treated bulbs were thoroughly washed in running tap water to remove the residual effect of the chemicals used.
steps.searchable_summary: wash treated bulbs to remove chemical residues

steps.description: The control and the treated bulbs were grown in the experimental plots in the randomized block design to raise M1 generation.
steps.searchable_summary: plant bulbs in randomized block design for M1 generation

methods.description: This method involves analyzing the pollen fertility and sterility of Urginea indica Kunth flowers raised from treated bulbs.

steps.description: Pollens were dusted on the glass slides for acetocarmine staining.
Stained pollen grains were considered to be fertile and unstained one as sterile.
steps.searchable_summary: determine pollen fertility based on staining

steps.description: The percentage pollen fertility and sterility was determined by the formulae.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate percentage of pollen fertility and sterility

steps.description: Shape of the pollen grains of treated flowers were determined by using Erdtman (1952) table based on the relation between polar axis and equational axis (P/E) of the pollen grains of Urginea indica Kunth.
steps.searchable_summary: determine pollen shape using Erdtman table
